text.title: Roadmap Design Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Roadmap Design. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Uncertainty low → timeline; medium → Now-Next-Later; high → outcome-themed.
rule.rationale: Uncertainty low → timeline; medium → Now-Next-Later; high → outcome-themed.

rule.statement: Maintain one internal source-of-truth; emit external derivative views — never copy-paste.
rule.rationale: Maintain one internal source-of-truth; emit external derivative views — never copy-paste.

rule.statement: External roadmap names themes + benefits; never specific metric targets (commitment risk).
rule.rationale: External roadmap names themes + benefits; never specific metric targets (commitment risk).

rule.statement: Internal roadmap: monthly review; external: quarterly publish.
rule.rationale: Internal roadmap: monthly review; external: quarterly publish.

rule.statement: Every roadmap carries an explicit not-doing list at the horizon scope.
rule.rationale: Every roadmap carries an explicit not-doing list at the horizon scope.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-roadmap-design.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
